title: Supervised Learning

prerequisites.text: Acceptance into the Master's program in Data Science or Statistics and Data Science, and/or statistical background deemed sufficient by Head of Department.

outline: Supervised learning covers statistical modelling tools for predicting or estimating relationships between predictor and target variables in complex data sets. This course covers: the bias-variance trade-off; regularisation; variable selection procedures; model evaluation and comparison. Modelling techniques include linear and non-linear regression; logistic regression; classification and regression trees; ensemble techniques like random forests and gradient boosting. The course also introduces Support Vector Machines, Neural Networks, and Deep Learning principles, with practical application in R.
assessment: Six quizzes (25%) and a take-home exam on each of three sections (75%).
